rationale: Two-premise Leibniz proof: take phi(z):=(t1=z); phi(t2) is the premise t1=t2 (already true), and def:eq-elim applied with the premise t2=t3 rewrites t2 to t3, yielding t1=t3. No reflexive instance is ever formed, so def:eq-refl is not invoked. This is the exact contrast the audit targets: eq-refl is load-bearing in thm:eq-sym (0006), where the single premise forces a reflexive seed, but ambient here because the second premise supplies the seed.
notes: Removal, not addition, so no licensing span applies. If the author intends a uniform {def:eq-elim, def:eq-refl} basis across all equality theorems as a stylistic convention, keep eq-refl; mathematically the transitivity proof does not use it. Low confidence by design -> route to focused re-run.
